metadata.description: RAGAS evaluation dataset for Patents Analyzer RAG system
metadata.corpus: 10 European Patent Applications on non-oriented electrical steel sheets

test_cases.question: In patent EP 2 390 376, the parameter RTi is used to control the ratio of Ti to C and N. What is the exact mathematical expression for RTi, and what are its claimed lower and upper bounds?
test_cases.ground_truth: RTi = [Ti] / (4 × ([C] + [N])), where [Ti], [C], and [N] are contents in mass%. The lower bound is 1 and the upper bound is 10. More preferably, RTi ≥ 2 (more preferably ≥ 3) and RTi ≤ 9 (more preferably ≤ 7).

test_cases.question: EP 1 816 226 defines two simultaneous compositional constraints as [Formula 1] and [Formula 2] that must be satisfied for REM oxysulfide formation and TiN coprecipitation. Reproduce both formulas exactly.
test_cases.ground_truth:
Formula 1: [REM]² × [O]² × [S] ≥ 1 × 10⁻¹⁵
Formula 2: ([REM]² × [O]² × [S]) ÷ ([Ti] × [N]) ≥ 1 × 10⁻¹⁰
where [REM], [O], [S], [Ti], and [N] are mass% of the respective elements.
test_cases.evidence: EP1816226_A1, paragraphs [0030], [0066], and the Claims section (Claim 1)

test_cases.question: In EP 2 278 034, the eddy current loss We10/400 is calculated using a dual frequency method. What is the exact calculation formula given in the patent for deriving We10/400 from two core loss measurements W1 and W2 at frequencies f1 and f2?
test_cases.ground_truth: We10/400 = (W2/f2 − W1/f1) / (f2 − f1) × 400 × 400. This is applied at a maximum magnetic flux density Bmax of 1.0 T.
